Ayushman Bharat PMJAY Eligibility Criteria 2026

Find out if you qualify for India's largest government-funded healthcare program providing Rs 5 lakh annual cashless health cover per family.

Fact-checked and updated for July 2026

What is the Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY Scheme?

What is the PMJAY full form? PMJAY stands for Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana, which is the flagship national health insurance scheme launched by the Government of India under the Ayushman Bharat initiative. It offers free, cashless medical treatment of up to ₹5 lakh per family per year at registered government and empaneled private hospitals. The scheme is designed to protect low-income households from catastrophic healthcare expenses, with no limit on family size, age, or gender.

Quick Checker Tool

Want a quick answer? Use our interactive checker tool to see if your family is eligible in under 2 minutes.

Start Eligibility Check

Rural Eligibility Rules

In rural areas, eligibility is based on your family's housing, income source, and household situation. You qualify if your household meets any of the following conditions:

Official CodeHousehold Situation / ConditionEligibility Status
D1Families living in a temporary, single-room house (kucha walls and roof).Eligible
D2Families with no working-age adults (no members aged 16 to 59).Eligible
D3Homes run by women, with no adult male member aged 16 to 59.Eligible
D4Families with a disabled member and no fit adult to support them.Eligible
D5Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) households.Eligible
D7Families with no land who earn their living mostly from manual daily-wage labor.Eligible

Additionally, families living in extreme poverty, manual scavengers, primitive tribal groups, and legally released bonded laborers are automatically eligible.

Urban Eligibility: Eligible Occupations

In cities and towns (urban areas), eligibility is based on the type of work you do. If the main earner of your family works in any of these informal jobs, you qualify:

  • Ragpickers, sweepers, and sanitation workers
  • Beggars or those surviving on charity
  • Domestic workers (maids, cooks, and home helpers)
  • Street vendors, hawkers, cobblers, and fruit/vegetable sellers
  • Construction workers, plumbers, masons, painters, and welders
  • Security guards, coolies, loaders, and manual helpers
  • Gardeners and sweepers
  • Artisans, tailors, weavers, and handicraft makers working from home
  • Drivers, conductors, rickshaw pullers, and cart pullers
  • Shop workers, delivery helpers, waiters, and peons
  • Washermen (dhobis) and mechanics

Who is Disqualified (Exclusions)?

Important: Even if your family meets the rural or urban conditions, you will not qualify for the scheme if you meet any of these disqualification (exclusion) rules:

Disqualification RuleDescriptionStatus
Motorized vehiclesOwning a motorized vehicle (like a motorcycle, scooter, car, auto-rickshaw, or motorboat).Excluded
Farming machineryOwning farming machinery like tractors or harvesters.Excluded
Kisan Credit Card (KCC) limitHaving a Kisan Credit Card (KCC) with a limit of more than ₹50,000.Excluded
Government jobHaving any family member working in a permanent government job.Excluded
Tax payersHaving any family member who pays income tax or professional tax.Excluded
Large landholdingOwning more than 5 acres of irrigated farmland, or over 7.5 acres of dry land.Excluded
Home appliancesHaving a refrigerator, an air conditioner, or a landline phone at home.Excluded

Eligibility for Senior Citizens (70 Years and Above)

All senior citizens aged 70 years and above are eligible for Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY health cover, regardless of their family income, land, or assets.

Key things to know:

  • Every senior citizen aged 70+ gets a separate, dedicated Ayushman Card.
  • They get a free health cover of ₹5 lakh per year, which is shared among the senior members of the family.
  • Seniors who already have other government health schemes (like CGHS or ECHS) can choose to either keep their existing scheme or switch to Ayushman Bharat.

Required Documents for Ayushman Card

To register or check your name in the official beneficiary list, keep the following documents ready:

  • Aadhaar Card: Used to verify your identity and biometrics (fingerprint or face scan).
  • Ration Card: Needed to verify the members of your family.
  • Active mobile number: To receive OTP verification codes on the official portal.
  • Address proof: Required only if your current address is different from your Aadhaar card.
  • Caste certificate: Needed if you are applying under the Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) category.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the full form of PMJAY?

The full form of PMJAY is Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana. It is the health insurance scheme under the Ayushman Bharat mission that provides Rs 5 lakh annual health coverage per eligible family.

How can I check my name in the official PMJAY list?

You can check your name on the official portal by using the Am I Eligible option with your mobile number, ration card number, HHD number, or name. You can also visit a Common Service Centre or empaneled hospital helpdesk.

Is there an income limit for Ayushman Bharat eligibility?

The scheme targets low-income and vulnerable households. Rural families must match SECC deprivation criteria, while urban households must fall into approved informal worker categories. Government employees and income-tax payers are excluded.

Can I apply for an Ayushman Card if I own a motorcycle?

Motorized vehicle ownership is generally treated as an exclusion signal under PM-JAY eligibility rules, so many households with a motorcycle will not qualify.

Is the PMJAY cover per person or per family?

The health cover is a family-floater benefit. The annual amount can be used by one member or shared across family members during the year.

14555
